title: Element Handling
description: Advanced techniques for handling different types of elements

questions.question: How do you handle file uploads in Playwright?
questions.explanation: setInputFiles() is used to upload files to file input elements.

questions.question: Complete the code to get element text content:
questions.explanation: textContent() or innerText() retrieves the text content of an element.

questions.question: Playwright can interact with elements inside iframes without switching context.
questions.explanation: You need to get the frame locator using page.frameLocator() to interact with iframe content.

questions.question: Which method retrieves an element's attribute value?
questions.explanation: getAttribute(name) returns the value of the specified attribute.

questions.question: Complete the code to interact with iframe content:
questions.explanation: frameLocator() creates a frame locator for interacting with iframe content.

questions.question: How do you check if an element is checked (checkbox/radio)?
questions.explanation: isChecked() returns true if a checkbox or radio button is checked.

questions.question: What's wrong with this iframe interaction?
options: Should use frameLocator instead of locator
questions.explanation: Use frameLocator() for iframe interactions, not regular locator().

questions.question: The inputValue() method works only on input, textarea, and select elements.
questions.explanation: inputValue() retrieves the value of input elements, textareas, and select elements.

questions.question: How do you get the bounding box of an element?
questions.explanation: boundingBox() returns the element's position and size information.

questions.question: What does this code do?
options: Sets input value directly in DOM
questions.explanation: evaluate() executes JavaScript in the browser context, directly setting the DOM value.
